[S2] " it shall not make me wink . i shall manage to keep the pieces . "

question: who knew about fishes that lived on land ?

[S2] indeed , so clever were they that they might have been hunting still if a terrible thing had not happened .

question: what did the fish tribe do after they came back from hunting expedition ?
answer: looked about for a nice , cool spot .
full_context_numbered:
[S0] one day the whole fish tribe came back very tired from a hunting expedition , and looked about for a nice , cool spot in which to pitch their camp .
[S1] it was very hot , and they thought that they could not find a more comfortable place than under the branches of a large tree which grew by the bank of a river .
[S2] so they made their fire to cook some food , right on the edge of a steep bank , which had a deep pool of water lying beneath it at the bottom .
[S3] while the food was cooking they all stretched themselves lazily out under the tree , and were just dropping off to sleep when a big black cloud which they had never noticed spread over the sun , and heavy drops of rain began to fall , so that the fire was almost put out , and that , you know , is a very serious thing in savage countries where they have no matches , for it is very hard to light it again .
[S4] to make matters worse , an icy wind began to blow , and the poor fishes were chilled right through their bodies .
[S5] ' this will never do , ' said thuggai , the oldest of the fish tribe .
[S6] ' we shall die of cold unless we can light the fire again , ' and he bade his sons rub two sticks together in the hope of kindling a flame , but though they rubbed till they were tired , not a spark could they produce .
evidence_context: [S0] one day the whole fish tribe came back very tired from a hunting expedition , and looked about for a nice , cool spot in which to pitch their camp .

question_zh: 鱼部落在狩猎远征回来后做了什么？
answer_zh: 寻找一个凉爽的好地方。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 有一天，整个鱼部落狩猎远征归来，非常疲惫，他们寻找一个凉爽的好地方来搭建营地。
[S1] 天气非常炎热，他们认为在河边一棵大树下找不到更舒适的地方了。
[S2] 于是他们在陡峭河岸的边缘生火做饭，河岸下方有一个深水潭。
[S3] 食物在烹饪时，他们都懒洋洋地躺在树下，正要入睡时，一片他们从未注意到的大黑云遮住了太阳，开始下起大雨，火几乎被浇灭。在野蛮国家，没有火柴，火被浇灭是非常严重的事情，因为很难重新点燃。
[S4] 更糟的是，一阵冰冷的风开始吹，可怜的鱼们全身发冷。
[S5] "这样不行，"鱼部落最年长的图盖说。
[S6] "除非我们能重新点燃火，否则我们会冻死。"他让儿子们摩擦两根木棍，希望能生火，但尽管他们摩擦到筋疲力尽，也没能产生一点火花。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 有一天，整个鱼部落狩猎远征归来，非常疲惫，他们寻找一个凉爽的好地方来搭建营地。

question: whose delight was it to play tricks on everybody, and particularly on the shepherds and the cowboys ?
full_context_numbered:
[S0] among the mountain pastures and valleys that lie in the centre of france there dwelt a mischievous kind of spirit .
[S1] it was this spirit 's delight to play tricks on everybody , and particularly on the shepherds and the cowboys .
[S2] they never knew when they were safe from him , as he could change himself into a man , woman or child , a stick , a goat , a ploughshare .
[S3] indeed , there was only one thing whose shape he could not take , and that was a needle .
[S4] at least , he could transform himself into a needle , but try as he might he never was able to imitate the hole .
[S5] every woman would have found him out at once , and this he knew .
[S6] now the hour oftenest chosen by this naughty sprite ( whom we will call puck ) for performing his pranks was about midnight .
[S7] this was just when the shepherds and cowherds , tired out with their long day 's work , were sound asleep .
[S8] then he would go into the cowsheds and unfasten the chains that fixed each beast in its own stall , and let them fall with a heavy clang to the ground .
[S9] the noise was so loud that it was certain to awaken the cowboys , however fatigued they might be .
[S10] they dragged themselves wearily to the stable to put back the chains .
[S11] but no sooner had they returned to their beds than the same thing happened again , and so on till the morning .
[S12] or perhaps puck would spend his night in plaiting together the manes and tails of two of the horses , so that it would take the grooms hours of labour to get them right in the morning .
[S13] puck , hidden among the hay in the loft , would peep out to watch them , enjoying himself amazingly all the time .

question_zh: 谁喜欢捉弄每个人，尤其是牧羊人和牛仔？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 在法国中部的山区牧场和山谷中，住着一种淘气的精灵。
[S1] 这个精灵喜欢捉弄每个人，尤其是牧羊人和牛仔。
[S2] 他们永远不知道什么时候能避开他，因为他可以变成男人、女人或孩子，一根棍子、一只山羊、一个犁头。
[S3] 事实上，只有一种东西的形状他无法模仿，那就是针。
[S4] 至少，他可以变成针，但无论怎么努力，他永远无法模仿针孔。
[S5] 任何女人都能立刻识破他，这一点他很清楚。
[S6] 现在，这个淘气的小精灵（我们称他为帕克）最常选择午夜时分来恶作剧。
[S7] 这正是牧羊人和牛仔们经过漫长一天的工作后熟睡的时候。
[S8] 然后他会走进牛棚，解开固定每头牛的链条，让它们重重地掉在地上。
[S9] 声音如此之大，肯定会惊醒牛仔们，无论他们有多累。
[S10] 他们疲惫地拖着身子去马厩重新挂上链条。
[S11] 但他们刚回到床上，同样的事情又发生了，如此反复直到早晨。
[S12] 或者帕克可能会花一晚上时间把两匹马的鬃毛和尾巴编在一起，这样早上马夫们要花几个小时才能解开。
[S13] 帕克藏在干草棚里，偷偷看着他们，一直乐不可支。
evidence_context_zh: [S2] 他们永远不知道什么时候能避开他，因为他可以变成男人、女人或孩子，一根棍子、一只山羊、一个犁头。

question_zh: 为什么人们无法知道自己何时能免受这种顽皮精灵的侵扰？
answer_zh: 因为他能变成男人、女人或小孩，一根棍子、一只山羊或一把犁。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 在法国中部的高山牧场和山谷中，居住着一种顽皮的精灵。

[S2] 他们永远无法知道自己何时能免受他的侵扰，因为他能变成男人、女人或小孩，一根棍子、一只山羊或一把犁。
[S3] 事实上，只有一种形态他无法模仿，那就是针。
[S4] 至少，他能变成针的样子，但无论怎么尝试，他都无法复制针孔。
[S5] 任何女人都能立刻识破他，这一点他自己也清楚。
[S6] 现在，这个淘气的小精灵（我们姑且称他为帕克）最常选择午夜时分来恶作剧。
[S7] 这正是牧羊人和牛仔们因一整天的工作而筋疲力尽、酣睡之时。
[S8] 这时他会溜进牛棚，解开每头牛固定在自己隔间里的锁链，让它们重重地咣当一声掉在地上。
[S9] 声响如此之大，无论牛仔们多么疲惫，都必定会被惊醒。
[S10] 他们疲惫不堪地拖着身子去牛棚重新拴好锁链。
[S11] 可刚回到床上，同样的事情又会发生，如此反复直到天亮。
[S12] 或者帕克会花一整夜把两匹马的鬃毛和尾巴编在一起，让马夫们清晨得花好几个小时才能解开。
[S13] 帕克藏在干草棚的草堆里偷看他们，始终乐不可支。

[S1] it happened that she fell ill , and when she felt that she must soon die , she sent for the king , and said , ' if you want to marry after my death , make no one queen unless she is just as beautiful as i am , and has just such golden hair as i have .
[S2] promise me this .
[S3] ' after the king had promised her this , she closed her eyes and died .
[S4] for a long time the king was not to be comforted , and he did not even think of taking a second wife .
[S5] at last his councillors said , ' the king must marry again , so that we may have a queen .
[S6] ' so messengers were sent far and wide to seek for a bride equal to the late queen in beauty .
[S7] but there was no one in the wide world , and if there had been she could not have had such golden hair .
[S8] then the messengers came home again , not having been able to find a queen .

[S0] the flax was in full bloom .
[S1] it had pretty little blue flowers , as delicate as the wings of a moth .
[S2] the sun shone on it and the showers watered it .
[S3] this was as good for the flax as it is for little children to be washed and then kissed by their mothers .
[S4] they look much prettier for it , and so did the flax .
[S5] " people say that i look exceedingly well , " said the flax , " and that i am so fine and long that i shall make a beautiful piece of linen . how fortunate i am ! it makes me so happy to know that something can be made of me . how the sunshine cheers me , and how sweet and refreshing is the rain ! my happiness overpowers me ! no one in the world can feel happier than i. "
evidence_context: [S1] it had pretty little blue flowers , as delicate as the wings of a moth .
difficulty_hint_zh: Easy：答案可以直接在文本中找到，并且最小证据集只有一个证据句。
evidence_reason: S1 directly states the color of the flax's flowers as blue.
question_zh: 亚麻的花是什么颜色的？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 亚麻正值盛花期。
[S1] 它开着漂亮的蓝色小花，纤薄如蛾翅。
[S2] 阳光照耀着它，雨水浇灌着它。
[S3] 这对亚麻来说就像孩子们被母亲洗净亲吻般美好。
[S4] 他们因此更显可爱，亚麻也是如此。
[S5] "人们说我长得极好，"亚麻说，"说我如此纤细修长，定能织成美丽的亚麻布。我多幸运啊！想到自己能有所用处，我就无比快乐。阳光多么鼓舞我，雨水多么甘甜清新！我的幸福让我不能自已！世上没人比我更幸福了。"
evidence_context_zh: [S1] 它开着漂亮的蓝色小花，纤薄如蛾翅。

question: where did the poor woman live ?
answer: in a wretched hut far away from the village .
full_context_numbered:
[S0] once upon a time there was a poor woman , who lived in a wretched hut far away from the village .
[S1] she had but little to bite and less to burn , so she sent her little boy to the forest to gather wood .
[S2] he skipped and leaped , and leaped and skipped , in order to keep warm , for it was a cold , gray autumn day , and whenever he had gathered a root or a branch to add to his bundle , he had to slap his arms against his shoulders , for the cold made his hands as red as the whortleberry bushes over which he walked .
[S3] when he had filled his barrow , and was wandering homeward , he crossed a field of stubble .
[S4] there he saw lying a jagged white stone .
[S5] " o , you poor old stone , how white and pale you are ! you must be freezing terribly ! "
[S6] said the boy ; took off his jacket , and laid it over the stone .
[S7] and when he came back home with his wood , his mother asked him how it was that he was going around in the autumn cold in his shirt - sleeves .
[S8] he told her that he had seen a jagged old stone , quite white and pale with the frost , and that he had given it his jacket .
[S9] " you fool , " said the woman , " do you think a stone can freeze ? and even if it had chattered with frost , still , charity begins at home . your clothes cost enough as it is , even when you do n't hang them on the stones out in the field!"
[S10] --and with that she drove the boy out again to fetch his jacket .
[S11] when he came to the stone , the stone had turned around , and had raised itself from the ground on one side .
evidence_context: [S0] once upon a time there was a poor woman , who lived in a wretched hut far away from the village .
difficulty_hint_zh: Easy：答案可以直接在文本中找到，并且最小证据集只有一个证据句。
evidence_reason: S0 explicitly states the woman lived in a wretched hut far from the village.
question_zh: 贫穷的女人住在哪里？
answer_zh: 远离村庄的破旧小屋里。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有个贫穷的女人，住在远离村庄的破旧小屋里。
[S1] 她缺衣少食，便让小男孩去森林拾柴。
[S2] 他在寒凉的秋日里蹦跳取暖，每当捡到树根或树枝时，都要拍打双臂——他的双手已冻得像越橘丛般通红。
[S3] 装满手推车返家时，他穿过一片麦茬地。
[S4] 看见地上有块嶙峋的白色石头。
[S5] "哦，可怜的老石头，你苍白成这样！肯定冻坏了吧！"
[S6] 男孩说着脱下夹克盖在石头上。
[S7] 回家后母亲质问为何秋寒中只穿衬衣。
[S8] 他解释说是给结霜的苍白石头披了衣服。
[S9] "蠢货，"女人说，"石头怎么会冷？就算它冻得打颤，施舍也该先顾自家。你的衣服本就金贵，更不该挂在野地石头上！"
[S10] 说着赶男孩去取回夹克。
[S11] 回到原地时，石头已翻转翘起。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 从前有个贫穷的女人，住在远离村庄的破旧小屋里。

full_context_zh:
[S0] 几座高山脚下曾有个小村庄，不远处两条道路交汇，一条向东一条向西。

question: how did the strong young farmer feel when he saw something shining floating in the air inside the gardens ?
full_context_numbered:

[S5] and whenever it drew its breath in the ball sank down again , and it caught it in its jaws .
[S6] and so it went on without a stop .

question_zh: 当强壮的年轻农夫看到花园里有什么发光的东西在空中漂浮时，他感觉如何？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有个强壮的年轻农夫，一天傍晚从集市回家很晚。

[S2] 突然，他看到花园里有什么发光的东西在空中漂浮，像一颗水晶球般闪耀。
[S3] 他感到惊讶，便爬上了花园的围墙，但视野内一个人也没有；他只看到远处有个像狗一样的东西，正望着月亮。
[S4] 每当它呼出气息时，一颗火球就从它嘴里冒出，升向月亮。
[S5] 每当它吸入气息时，火球又降下来，被它用嘴接住。
[S6] 就这样周而复始，不停歇。

full_context_zh:

[S2] 里面有一张桌子，摆满了各种好吃的食物。
[S3] 还有一张床，床上躺着一条大蛇。
[S4] 蛇对女孩说：“如果你想坐，就坐下吧！如果你想吃，就吃吧！如果你想躺到床上，就躺下吧！但如果你不想，那就别这么做。”
[S5] 于是女孩什么都没做。
[S6] 最后蛇说：“现在有些人要来，想和你跳舞。但别跟他们去。”
[S7] 很快，那些人来了，想和女孩跳舞；但她完全不听。

question_zh: 国王因为妻子去世且深爱着她而做了什么？
answer_zh: 为王后哀悼了很长时间。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有位国王，他的妻子去世了，但他有个善良美丽的女儿，世上无人能比她更温柔可爱。

[S2] 继母和女儿嫉妒国王的女儿，因为她太美丽；但只要国王在家，她们就不敢伤害她，因为国王非常疼爱她。
[S3] 但过了一段时间，国王开始与另一位国王交战，出征去了。
[S4] 于是王后认为可以为所欲为，她让国王的女儿挨饿，到处殴打推搡她。
[S5] 最后连其他待遇都算奢侈，她只能去放牛。
[S6] 于是她带着牛群，在森林或山丘上放牧。
[S7] 她几乎没有食物，变得苍白消瘦，多数时候悲伤哭泣。
[S8] 牛群里有头蓝色的大公牛，总是保持整洁干净，经常来到国王女儿身边让她挠痒。

question: who did the king meet while hunting one day ?
answer: a man whose head was out through his cap , whose elbows and knees were out through his clothing , and whose toes were out through his shoes .
full_context_numbered:
[S0] there was a king in his own time in erin , and he went hunting one day .
[S1] the king met a man whose head was out through his cap , whose elbows and knees were out through his clothing , and whose toes were out through his shoes .
[S2] the man went up to the king , gave him a blow on the face , and drove three teeth from his mouth .
[S3] the same blow put the king 's head in the dirt .
[S4] when he rose from the earth , the king went back to his castle , and lay down sick and sorrowful .
[S5] the king had three sons , and their names were ur , arthur , and lawn dyarrig .
[S6] the three were at school that day , and came home in the evening .
[S7] the father sighed when the sons were coming in .
[S8] " what is wrong with our father ? " asked the eldest .
[S9] " your father is sick on his bed , " said the mother .
[S10] the three sons went to their father and asked what was on him .
[S11] " a strong man that i met to - day gave me a blow in the face , put my head in the dirt , and knocked three teeth from my mouth . what would you do to him if you met him ? " asked the father of the eldest son .
[S12] " if i met that man , " replied ur , " i would make four parts of him between four horses . "
[S13] " you are my son , " said the king .
[S14] " what would you do if you met him ? "
[S15] asked he then as he turned to the second son .
[S16] " if i had a grip on that man i would burn him between four fires . "
[S17] " you , too , are my son . what would you do ? " asked the king of lawn dyarrig .
evidence_context: [S1] the king met a man whose head was out through his cap , whose elbows and knees were out through his clothing , and whose toes were out through his shoes .

question_zh: 国王某天打猎时遇见了谁？

[S7] 儿子们进门时父亲正叹息。
[S8] "父亲怎么了？"长子问。
[S9] "你们父亲卧病在床。"母亲答。
[S10] 三兄弟询问父亲遭遇。
[S11] "今日有个壮汉打我脸、按我头入土、击落我三颗牙。若你们遇见他会怎么做？"国王问长子。
[S12] "若我遇见那人，"乌尔答，"必用四匹马将他分尸。"
[S13] "不愧是我儿。"国王说。
[S14] 他转向次子问："你会怎么做？"
[S15] "若抓住那人，我必用四面火堆烧死他。"
[S16] "你也是我儿。"国王又问劳恩·迪亚瑞格："你呢？"

question: what happened after morraha rose in the morning ?
answer: he washed rose in the morning , and washed his hands and face , and said his prayers , and ate his food ; and he asked god to prosper the day for him . he went down to the brink of the sea , and he saw a currach , short and green , coming towards him . in it there was but one youthful champion , and he playing hurly from prow to stern of the currach .

[S1] he asked god to prosper the day for him ; and he went down to the brink of the sea , and he saw a currach , short and green , coming towards him .
[S2] in it there was but one youthful champion , and he playing hurly from prow to stern of the currach .
[S3] he had a hurl of gold and a ball of silver .
[S4] he stopped not until the currach was in on the shore .
[S5] he drew her up on the green grass , and put fastening on her for a day and a year , whether he should be there all that time , or should only be on land for an hour by the clock .
[S6] and morraha saluted the young man in words intelligent , intelligible , such as were spoken at that time .
[S7] the other saluted him in the same fashion , and asked him would he play a game of cards with him ; and morraha said he had not the wherewithal .
[S8] the other answered that he was never without a candle or the making of it .
[S9] he put his hand in his pocket and drew out a table and two chairs and a pack of cards , and they sat down on the chairs and went to the card - playing .
[S10] the first game morraha won , and the slender red champion bade him make his claim .
[S11] he said that the land above him should be filled with stock of sheep in the morning .
[S12] it was well , and he played no second game , but home he went .
[S13] the next day morraha went to the brink of the sea , and the young man came in the currach and asked him would he play cards .
[S14] they played , and morraha won .
[S15] and the young man bade him make his claim .
[S16] he said that the land above should be filled with cattle in the morning .
[S17] it was well , and he played no other game , but went home .

[S0] morraha rose in the morning , and washed his hands and face , and said his prayers , and ate his food .

question_zh: 莫拉哈早上起床后发生了什么？
answer_zh: 他早上起床后，洗了手和脸，做了祷告，吃了饭；他祈求上帝让他一天顺利。他走到海边，看到一艘短小的绿色小船向他驶来。船上只有一个年轻的勇士，他从船头到船尾玩着曲棍球。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 莫拉哈早上起床后，洗了手和脸，做了祷告，吃了饭。
[S1] 他祈求上帝让他一天顺利；他走到海边，看到一艘短小的绿色小船向他驶来。
[S2] 船上只有一个年轻的勇士，他从船头到船尾玩着曲棍球。
[S3] 他有一根金球棍和一个银球。
[S4] 他没有停下，直到小船靠岸。
[S5] 他把船拖上绿草地，为船系上绳索，无论他在那里待上一天还是一年，或者只在岸上待一个小时。
[S6] 莫拉哈用当时通用的智慧言语向年轻人致意。
[S7] 对方以同样的方式回礼，并问他是否愿意和他玩一局牌；莫拉哈说他没有什么可赌的。
[S8] 对方回答说，他从不缺蜡烛或制作蜡烛的材料。
[S9] 他把手伸进口袋，拿出一张桌子、两把椅子和一副牌，他们坐在椅子上开始玩牌。
[S10] 第一局莫拉哈赢了，瘦高的红发勇士让他提出要求。
[S11] 他说第二天早上他上面的土地应该满是羊群。
[S12] 很好，他没有玩第二局，就回家了。
[S13] 第二天莫拉哈又来到海边，年轻人乘着小船来问他是否愿意玩牌。
[S14] 他们玩了，莫拉哈赢了。
[S15] 年轻人让他提出要求。
[S16] 他说第二天早上土地应该满是牛群。
[S17] 很好，他没有再玩其他游戏，就回家了。
evidence_context_zh:
[S0] 莫拉哈早上起床后，洗了手和脸，做了祷告，吃了饭。
[S1] 他祈求上帝让他一天顺利；他走到海边，看到一艘短小的绿色小船向他驶来。

question: which daughter was made to do all the work around the house ?

question: who was very big and strong, but the clumsiest creature you could imagine ?
full_context_numbered:
[S0] once upon a time there was a youth called moti , who was very big and strong , but the clumsiest creature you can imagine .
[S1] so clumsy was he that he was always putting his great feet into the bowls of sweet milk or curds which his mother set out on the floor to cool , always smashing , upsetting , breaking , until at last his father said to him : ' here , moti , are fifty silver pieces which are the savings of years ; take them and go and make your living or your fortune if you can .
[S2] ' then moti started off one early spring morning with his thick staff over his shoulder , singing gaily to himself as he walked along .
[S3] in one way and another he got along very well until a hot evening when he came to a certain city where he entered the travellers ' ' serai ' or inn to pass the night .
[S4] now a serai , you must know , is generally just a large square enclosed by a high wall with an open colonnade along the inside all round to accommodate both men and beasts , and with perhaps a few rooms in towers at the corners for those who are too rich or too proud to care about sleeping by their own camels and horses .
[S5] moti , of course , was a country lad and had lived with cattle all his life , and he was n't rich and he was n't proud , so he just borrowed a bed from the innkeeper , set it down beside an old buffalo who reminded him of home , and in five minutes was fast asleep .
evidence_context: [S0] once upon a time there was a youth called moti , who was very big and strong , but the clumsiest creature you can imagine .

question_zh: 谁非常高大强壮，却是你能想象到的最笨拙的生物？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有个叫莫提的年轻人，他非常高大强壮，却是你能想象到的最笨拙的生物。
[S1] 他笨拙到总是把大脚踩进母亲放在地上冷却的甜牛奶或凝乳碗里，总是打翻、弄碎、破坏，直到最后父亲对他说：‘莫提，这里有攒了多年的五十银币；拿着它们去谋生吧，如果可能的话去闯荡你的前程。
[S2]’ 于是莫提在一个早春的早晨出发了，肩上扛着粗棍，边走边快活地哼着歌。
[S3] 他一路顺遂，直到一个炎热的傍晚来到某座城市，进了旅客的‘萨莱’即客栈过夜。
[S4] 要知道，萨莱通常就是个由高墙围住的大广场，内侧有开放式柱廊供人和牲畜歇息，角落的塔楼里或许有几间房，给那些太富有或太高傲、不愿睡在自己骆驼和马匹旁边的人住。
[S5] 莫提当然是个乡下小伙，一生与牲畜为伴，既不富有也不高傲，所以他只向店主借了张床，放在一头让他想起家乡的老水牛旁边，五分钟内就睡熟了。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 从前有个叫莫提的年轻人，他非常高大强壮，却是你能想象到的最笨拙的生物。

question_zh: 莫提是如何笨拙的？
answer_zh: 总是把大脚踩进甜牛奶或凝乳碗里。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有个叫莫提的年轻人，他非常高大强壮，却是你能想象到的最笨拙的生物。
[S1] 他笨拙到总是把大脚踩进母亲放在地上冷却的甜牛奶或凝乳碗里，总是打翻、弄碎、破坏，直到最后父亲对他说：‘莫提，这里有攒了多年的五十银币；拿着它们去谋生吧，如果可能的话去闯荡你的前程。
[S2]’ 于是莫提在一个早春的早晨出发了，肩上扛着粗棍，边走边快活地哼着歌。
[S3] 他一路顺遂，直到一个炎热的傍晚来到某座城市，进了旅客的‘萨莱’即客栈过夜。
[S4] 要知道，萨莱通常就是个由高墙围住的大广场，内侧有开放式柱廊供人和牲畜歇息，角落的塔楼里或许有几间房，给那些太富有或太高傲、不愿睡在自己骆驼和马匹旁边的人住。
[S5] 莫提当然是个乡下小伙，一生与牲畜为伴，既不富有也不高傲，所以他只向店主借了张床，放在一头让他想起家乡的老水牛旁边，五分钟内就睡熟了。

question: what happened in the days when murtough mac erca was in the high kingship of ireland ?
answer: the country was divided between the old beliefs of paganism and the new doctrines of the christian teaching .
full_context_numbered:
[S0] in the days when murtough mac erca was in the high kingship of ireland , the country was divided between the old beliefs of paganism and the new doctrines of the christian teaching .
[S1] part held with the old creed and part with the new , and the thought of the people was troubled between them , for they knew not which way to follow and which to forsake .
[S2] the faith of their forefathers clung close around them , holding them by many fine and tender threads of memory and custom and tradition ; yet still the new faith was making its way , and every day it spread wider and wider through the land .
[S3] the family of murtough had joined itself to the christian faith , and his three brothers were bishops and abbots of the church , but murtough himself remained a pagan , for he was a wild and lawless prince , and the peaceful teachings of the christian doctrine , with its forgiveness of enemies , pleased him not at all .
[S4] fierce and cruel was his life , filled with dark deeds and bloody wars , and savage and tragic was his death , as we shall hear .
[S5] now murtough was in the sunny summer palace of cletty , which cormac , son of art , had built for a pleasure house on the brink of the slow - flowing boyne , near the fairy brugh of angus the ever young , the god of youth and beauty .
[S6] a day of summer was that day , and the king came forth to hunt on the borders of the brugh , with all his boon companions around him .
[S7] but when the high - noon came the sun grew hot , and the king sat down to rest upon the fairy mound , and the hunt passed on beyond him , and he was left alone .
evidence_context: [S0] in the days when murtough mac erca was in the high kingship of ireland , the country was divided between the old beliefs of paganism and the new doctrines of the christian teaching .
difficulty_hint_zh: Easy：答案可以直接在文本中找到，并且最小证据集只有一个证据句。
evidence_reason: S0 directly states the division in the country.
question_zh: 在穆尔图·麦克·埃尔卡担任爱尔兰至高王期间发生了什么？
answer_zh: 国家分裂于异教的古老信仰与基督教新教义之间。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 在穆尔图·麦克·埃尔卡担任爱尔兰至高王期间，国家分裂于异教的古老信仰与基督教新教义之间。
[S1] 部分人坚持旧信仰，部分人皈依新信仰，人们的思想因此困扰，因为他们不知道该追随哪条路又该放弃哪条。
[S2] 祖先的信仰通过许多美好而温柔的回忆、习俗和传统的丝线紧紧缠绕着他们；然而新信仰仍在推进，每天在全国传播得越来越广。
[S3] 穆尔图的家族已皈依基督教，他的三个兄弟是教会的主教和院长，但穆尔图本人仍是异教徒，因为他是个狂野不羁的王子，基督教义中关于宽恕敌人的和平教义根本不合他意。
[S4] 他的一生凶猛残酷，充满黑暗行径和血腥战争，他的死亡也野蛮而悲惨，我们将会听到。
[S5] 当时穆尔图在克莱提的夏日宫殿里，这座宫殿是艺术之子科马克建造的游乐行宫，位于缓缓流淌的博因河畔，靠近青春与美丽之神安格斯永恒的仙丘。
[S6] 那是个夏日，国王带着所有亲密伙伴来到仙丘边界狩猎。
[S7] 但正午时分太阳变得炽热，国王坐在仙丘上休息，狩猎队伍越过他继续前进，他独自留了下来。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 在穆尔图·麦克·埃尔卡担任爱尔兰至高王期间，国家分裂于异教的古老信仰与基督教新教义之间。

question_zh: 谁背着房子跑？

question: what was the little girl like ?
full_context_numbered:
[S0] once upon a time there was little girl , pretty and dainty .
[S1] but in summer time she was obliged to go barefooted because she was poor , and in winter she had to wear large wooden shoes , so that her little instep grew quite red .

[S3] they were clumsy , but she meant well , for they were intended for the little girl , whose name was karen .
[S4] karen received the shoes and wore them for the first time on the day of her mother 's funeral .
[S5] they were certainly not suitable for mourning ; but she had no others , and so she put her bare feet into them and walked behind the humble coffin .
[S6] just then a large old carriage came by , and in it sat an old lady ; she looked at the little girl , and taking pity on her , said to the clergyman , " look here , if you will give me the little girl , i will take care of her . "
evidence_context: [S0] once upon a time there was little girl , pretty and dainty .

question_zh: 小女孩是什么样子的？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 从前有一个小女孩，漂亮又精致。

[S3] 鞋子很笨拙，但她是出于好意，因为这是给那个叫卡伦的小女孩的。

[S5] 它们当然不适合哀悼；但她没有别的鞋，于是她光着脚穿上它们，跟在简陋的棺材后面走。

evidence_context_zh: [S0] 从前有一个小女孩，漂亮又精致。

question: who lived down by the iron forge ?
full_context_numbered:
[S0] long ago a poor widow woman lived down by the iron forge near enniscorthy , and she was so poor , she had no clothes to put on her son .
[S1] so , she used to fix him in the ash - hole , near the fire , and pile the warm ashes about him .
[S2] accordingly , as he grew up , she sunk the pit deeper .
[S3] at last , by hook or by crook , she got a goat - skin , and fastened it round his waist , and he felt quite grand , and took a walk down the street .
[S4] so says she to him next morning , " tom , you thief , you never done any good yet , and six - foot high , and past nineteen . take that rope and bring me a bresna from the wood . "
[S5] " never say't twice , mother , "
[S6] says tom ; " here goes . " when he had it gathered and tied , what should come up but a big joiant , nine - foot high , and made a lick of a club at him .
[S7] well become tom , he jumped a - one side and picked up a ram - pike .
[S8] the first crack he gave the big fellow he made him kiss the clod .
[S9] " if you have e'er a prayer , " says tom , " now 's the time to say it , before i make brishe of you . "
[S10] " i have no prayers , " says the giant , " but if you spare my life i 'll give you that club . as long as you keep from sin you 'll win every battle you ever fight with it . "
[S11] tom made no bones about letting him off .
[S12] as soon as he got the club in his hands he sat down on the bresna and gave it a tap with the kippeen , and says , " bresna , i had a great trouble gathering you , and run the risk of my life for you . the least you can do is to carry me home . "
[S13] and , sure enough , the wind of the word was all it wanted .
[S14] it went off through the wood , groaning and cracking till it came to the widow 's door .

question_zh: 谁住在铁匠铺附近？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 很久以前，一位贫穷的寡妇住在恩尼斯科西附近的铁匠铺旁，她穷得连给儿子穿的衣服都没有。
[S1] 于是她常把他安置在火炉旁的灰坑里，用温暖的灰烬盖住他。
[S2] 随着他长大，她把坑挖得更深。
[S3] 最后，她设法弄到一块山羊皮，系在他腰间，他觉得自己很神气，便走到街上去。
[S4] 第二天早上她对他说：“汤姆，你这懒鬼，都六英尺高，过了十九岁，还没干过一件正事。拿上绳子去林子里给我砍根柴火。”
[S5] “不用再说第二遍，妈妈，”
[S6] 汤姆说；“这就去。”当他捆好柴火时，突然出现一个九英尺高的巨人，挥着大棒打向他。
[S7] 汤姆敏捷地跳到一旁，捡起一根木桩。
[S8] 他第一下就打倒了那个大个子。
[S9] “如果你有什么遗言，”汤姆说，“趁我还没把你打成碎片，快说吧。”
[S10] “我没有遗言，”巨人说，“但如果你饶我一命，我就把这根棒子给你。只要你不犯罪，用它战斗就永远不会输。”
[S11] 汤姆毫不犹豫地放了他。
[S12] 他一拿到棒子就坐在柴火上，用小木棍轻敲它说：“柴火啊，我费了好大劲才把你捆好，还差点送命。你至少该把我送回家。”
[S13] 果然，这句话刚说完。
[S14] 柴火就嘎吱作响地穿过树林，直到寡妇的门前。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 很久以前，一位贫穷的寡妇住在恩尼斯科西附近的铁匠铺旁，她穷得连给儿子穿的衣服都没有。

[S12] answer that thou sawest it , but be sure thou meetest me in the morning at this place .
[S13] '

question_zh: 当泰瑟镇国王的儿子抵达大战时，谁正在战斗？
answer_zh: 一条蛇和一只巨大的黑色乌鸦。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 地球上所有生物与空中飞鸟之间将爆发一场大战。
[S1] 消息传开后，泰瑟镇国王的儿子说，战斗打响时他会到场观战，并带回谁将成为国王的消息。
[S2] 尽管如此，他还是差点迟到，所有战斗都已结束，只剩最后一场——一条蛇与一只巨大黑鸦之间的对决。
[S3] 双方激烈搏斗，但最终蛇证明了自己更强壮，若非王子拔剑一击斩断蛇头，它本会缠住乌鸦的脖子直至其死亡。
[S4] 乌鸦见敌人已死，感激地说：'为报答今日恩情，我将让你见识奇景。
[S5] 现在请抓住我的双翼根部。
[S6] '王子照做后，乌鸦未停飞前，他们已越过七座山峰、七道峡谷和七片荒原。
[S7] '看见远处那房子了吗？
[S8] '乌鸦最终说道。
[S9] '直走过去，我姐姐住在那里，她会热情款待你。
[S10] 若她问，"你可曾参加百鸟之战？"
[S11] 答曰参加；若问，"可曾见我化身？"
[S12] 答曰见过，但明晨务必在此与我相会。
[S13] '
evidence_context_zh: [S2] 尽管如此，他还是差点迟到，所有战斗都已结束，只剩最后一场——一条蛇与一只巨大黑鸦之间的对决。

full_context_numbered:
[S0] at the time when the animals reigned in the earth , they had killed all the people but a girl and her little brother .
[S1] these two were living in fear , in an out - of - the - way place .
[S2] the boy was a perfect little pigmy , and never grew beyond the size of a mere infant .
[S3] the girl increased with her years , so that the task of providing food and shelter fell wholly upon her .
[S4] she went out daily to get wood for the lodge - fire , and she took her little brother with her that no mishap might befall him .
[S5] he was too little to leave alone .
[S6] a big bird , of a mischievous disposition , might have flown away with him .
[S7] she made him a bow and arrows , and said to him one day , " my little brother , i will leave you behind where i have been gathering the wood . you must hide yourself , and you will soon see the snow - birds come and pick the worms out of the logs which i have piled up . shoot one of them and bring it home . "
[S8] he obeyed her , and tried his best to kill one , but he came home unsuccessful .
[S9] his sister told him that he must not despair , but try again the next day .
evidence_context: [S4] she went out daily to get wood for the lodge - fire , and she took her little brother with her that no mishap might befall him .
difficulty_hint_zh: Easy：答案可以直接在文本中找到，并且最小证据集只有一个证据句。
evidence_reason: S4 directly states the girl went out daily to get wood for the lodge-fire.
question_zh: 女孩每天做什么？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 在动物统治大地的时代，它们杀光了所有人，只剩一个女孩和她的小弟弟。
[S1] 两人在偏僻处过着提心吊胆的生活。
[S2] 男孩是个十足的侏儒，始终只有婴儿大小。
[S3] 女孩则逐年长大，因此获取食物和住所的重担完全落在她肩上。
[S4] 她每天外出为小屋的炉火拾柴，并带着弟弟以防不测。
[S5] 他太小了不能独处。
[S6] 顽劣的大鸟可能会把他叼走。
[S7] 她给他做了弓箭，有天对他说："弟弟，我要把你留在我拾柴的地方。你必须躲好，很快就会看到雪雀来啄食我堆起的木头里的虫子。射一只带回家。"
[S8] 他听从了，竭力想射中一只，却空手而归。
[S9] 姐姐告诉他不要灰心，明天再试。
evidence_context_zh: [S4] 她每天外出为小屋的炉火拾柴，并带着弟弟以防不测。

question: who married a prince ?
full_context_numbered:
[S0] dectera , one of the sisters of conchubar mac nessa , married a prince whose patrimony lay along the shores of the muirnict , and whose capital was dun dalgan .
[S1] they had one child , a boy , whom they named setanta .
[S2] as soon as setanta was able to understand the stories and conversation of those around him , he evinced a passion for arms and the martial life , which was so premature and violent as to surprise all who knew him .
[S3] his thoughts for ever ran on the wars and achievements of the red branch .
[S4] he knew all the knights by name , the appearance and bearing of each , and what deeds of valour they had severally performed .
[S5] emain macha , the capital of the clanna rury , was never out of his mind .
[S6] he saw for ever before his mind its moats and ramparts , its gates and bridges , its streets filled with martial men , its high - raised duns and raths , its branching roads , over which came the tributes of wide ulla to the high king .
[S7] he had seen his father 's tribute driven thither , and had even longed to be one of the four - footed beasts that he beheld wending their way to the wondrous city .
[S8] but , above all , he delighted to be told of the great school where the young nobles of ulster were taught martial exercises and the military art , under the superintendence of chosen knights and of the high king himself .
[S9] of the several knights he had his own opinion , and had already resolved to accept no one as his instructor save fergus mac roy , tanist of ulster .
evidence_context: [S0] dectera , one of the sisters of conchubar mac nessa , married a prince whose patrimony lay along the shores of the muirnict , and whose capital was dun dalgan .

question_zh: 谁嫁给了王子？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 康丘巴·麦克·内萨的姐妹之一德克特拉，嫁给了一位王子，其封地沿穆尔尼克特海岸延伸，首府是邓达尔甘。
[S1] 他们有个儿子，取名瑟坦达。
[S2] 当瑟坦达能听懂周围人的故事和谈话时，就表现出对武器和军旅生活的狂热，这种早熟而强烈的兴趣令所有认识他的人惊讶。
[S3] 他的思绪永远萦绕在红枝骑士团的战争与功绩上。
[S4] 他知道所有骑士的名字、样貌举止，以及他们各自的英勇事迹。
[S5] 红枝族首府埃曼·马查从未离开过他的脑海。
[S6] 他眼前永远浮现着它的壕沟城墙、城门桥梁、满是武士的街道、高耸的堡垒要塞、四通八达的道路——乌尔拉各地的贡品正通过这些道路运往至高王。
[S7] 他曾目睹父亲的贡品被驱赶前往那里，甚至渴望成为那些通往神奇城市的四足野兽之一。
[S8] 但最令他神往的，是听说乌尔斯特年轻贵族们在精选骑士和至高王亲自监督下学习武艺与军事艺术的大学校。
[S9] 对各路骑士他有自己的见解，并已决定只接受乌尔斯特摄政王弗格斯·麦克·罗伊作为导师。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 康丘巴·麦克·内萨的姐妹之一德克特拉，嫁给了一位王子，其封地沿穆尔尼克特海岸延伸，首府是邓达尔甘。

question_zh: 王子的祖产位于何处？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 康丘巴·麦克·内萨的姐妹之一德克特拉嫁给了一位王子，其祖产位于muirnict海岸沿线，首都是敦达尔甘。
[S1] 他们有一个孩子，是个男孩，取名为塞坦塔。
[S2] 当塞坦塔能听懂周围人的故事和谈话时，他就表现出对武器和军事生活的热情，这种热情如此早熟且强烈，令所有认识他的人都感到惊讶。
[S3] 他的思绪永远围绕着红枝骑士团的战争和成就。
[S4] 他知道所有骑士的名字、每个人的外貌和举止，以及他们各自所完成的英勇事迹。
[S5] 埃曼·马查，克朗纳·鲁里的首都，从未离开过他的脑海。
[S6] 他永远在脑海中看到它的护城河和城墙、城门和桥梁、充满武士的街道、高耸的堡垒和土垒、分支的道路，以及来自广阔乌拉的贡品沿着这些道路运往至高王。
[S7] 他曾看到父亲的贡品被驱赶到那里，甚至渴望成为那些四足野兽之一，看着它们蜿蜒前行，前往那座神奇的城市。
[S8] 但最重要的是，他喜欢听人讲述那所伟大的学校，在那里，乌尔斯特的年轻贵族们在精选骑士和至高王本人的监督下学习军事训练和军事艺术。
[S9] 对于几位骑士，他有自己的看法，并已决定只接受乌尔斯特的塔尼斯特——弗格斯·麦克·罗伊作为他的导师。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 康丘巴·麦克·内萨的姐妹之一德克特拉嫁给了一位王子，其祖产位于muirnict海岸沿线，首都是敦达尔甘。

question_zh: 谁不太关心这个儿子？
full_context_zh:
[S0] 很久以前有一个人，那是很久以前的事了，如果那时他在，现在他就不在了。
[S1] 他结了婚，妻子去世了，他和第一任妻子只有一个儿子。
[S2] 然后他娶了第二任妻子。
[S3] 这第二任妻子不太关心这个儿子，他被迫到山上去，远离家，照顾牛群。
[S4] 山上的牛群中有一头斑纹公牛，有一天，这个男孩非常饥饿，斑纹公牛听到他在抱怨并搓着双手。
[S5] 公牛走到他身边，对他说：“你饿了，但把我的角取下来放在地上。把手伸进角原来的地方，你会找到食物。”
[S6] 他听到后走到公牛身边，抓住角，扭动它，角就掉在他手里。
[S7] 他把角放在地上，把手伸进去，拿出了食物、饮料和一块桌布。
[S8] 他把桌布铺在地上，摆上食物和饮料，然后吃饱喝足。
[S9] 当他吃饱喝足后，他把桌布放回去，把角也放回原来的位置。
evidence_context_zh: [S4] 这第二任妻子不太关心这个儿子，他被迫到山上去，远离家，照顾牛群。

[S0] 瓦萨莫和他的父母住在东北方遥远的一个大海湾岸边。
[S1] 一天，当鱼群开始丰盛的季节，瓦萨莫的母亲对他说，“儿子，我希望你能去那边的岬角看看能不能给我弄些鱼来；叫你的表兄陪你去。”
[S2] 他照做了。
[S3] 他们出发了，下午时分到达了捕鱼的地方。
[S4] 表兄年长些，负责照看渔网，他们在附近扎营，用桦树皮搭了个棚子过夜。
[S5] 他们生起篝火，坐着聊天时，月亮升起来了。
[S6] 湖面平静无风。
[S7] 天空万里无云。
[S8] 瓦萨莫望向湖中的渔网，发现那些小黑点——其实是浮标——已经消失了。
[S9] “表兄，”他说，“我们去看看渔网吧；也许我们运气不错。”
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 瓦萨莫和他的父母住在东北方遥远的一个大海湾岸边。

question: what did the prince do when he heard the sound of someone crying in the ditch ?
answer: dismounted from his horse , and stepped along in the direction the sound came from .
full_context_numbered:
[S0] a young prince was riding one day through a meadow that stretched for miles in front of him , when he came to a deep open ditch .
[S1] he was turning aside to avoid it , when he heard the sound of someone crying in the ditch .
[S2] he dismounted from his horse , and stepped along in the direction the sound came from .
[S3] to his astonishment he found an old woman , who begged him to help her out of the ditch .
[S4] the prince bent down and lifted her out of her living grave , asking her at the same time how she had managed to get there .
[S5] ' my son , ' answered the old woman , ' i am a very poor woman , and soon after midnight i set out for the neighbouring town in order to sell my eggs in the market on the following morning ; but i lost my way in the dark , and fell into this deep ditch , where i might have remained for ever but for your kindness .
[S6] '

question_zh: 当王子听到沟渠里传来哭声时，他做了什么？
answer_zh: 从马上下来，朝着声音传来的方向走去。
full_context_zh:

[S2] 他从马上下来，朝着声音传来的方向走去。
[S3] 令他惊讶的是，他发现一位老妇人，恳求他把她从沟渠里救出来。
[S4] 王子弯下腰将她从活坟墓中拉出，同时询问她是怎么掉进去的。
[S5] '我的孩子，'老妇人回答，'我是个非常贫穷的女人，午夜过后不久我就出发去邻近的城镇，准备第二天早上在市场上卖鸡蛋；但我在黑暗中迷了路，掉进了这条深沟，如果不是你的善心，我可能永远待在这里了。
[S6] '
evidence_context_zh: [S2] 他从马上下来，朝着声音传来的方向走去。

question_zh: 阿戈·达·高达因用那只脚攻击一位可敬的巫医而受到什么惩罚？

[S3] 达·高达在自己的领域也相当重要，因为他生活极为讲究，拥有自己的木屋，以及一个从前门门槛向西延伸数百英里（随他丈量）的庭院。
[S4] 尽管他有权主张这片广阔的土地，他仍告诫女儿不要远离家门，否则定会被偷走，因为他确信野牛之王日夜潜伏在附近等待抓她。
evidence_context_zh: [S0] 作为对曾用那只脚攻击一位可敬巫医的惩罚，阿戈·达·高达的一条腿被弯曲绑在大腿处，因此他不得不靠单脚跳跃前行。

question: what happened when one of the beans fell out and lay near the straw ?
answer: soon a red - hot coal jumped out of the fire and joined the pair .

[S0] there lived in a certain village a poor old woman who had collected a mess of beans , and was going to cook them .

[S5] the straw began first , and said , " dear friends , how do you come here ? "
[S6] the coal answered , " i jumped out of the fire by great good luck , or i should certainly have met with my death . i should have been burned to ashes . "
[S7] the bean said , " i too have come out of it with a whole skin . if the old woman had kept me in the pot i should have been cooked into a soft mass like my comrades . "
[S8] " nor should i have met with a better fate , " said the straw .
[S9] " the old woman has turned my brothers into fire and smoke , sixty of them she took up at once and deprived of life . very luckily i managed to slip through her fingers . "
[S10] " what had we better do now ? " said the coal .
[S11] " i think , " answered the bean , " that as we have been so lucky as to escape with our lives , we will join in good fellowship together . lest any more bad fortune should happen to us here , we will go abroad into foreign lands . "

question_zh: 当一颗豆子掉出来并躺在稻草旁边时，发生了什么？
answer_zh: 很快一块炽热的煤从火中跳出来加入了它们。
full_context_zh:
[S0] 某个村庄里住着一位贫穷的老妇人，她收集了一些豆子，准备煮它们。
[S1] 于是她在炉灶上生了火。
[S2] 为了让火烧得更旺，她放了一把稻草。
[S3] 当豆子在锅里开始冒泡时，一颗豆子掉了出来，未被注意到地躺在了一根稻草旁边。
[S4] 很快一块炽热的煤从火中跳出来加入了它们。
[S5] 稻草先开口说：“亲爱的朋友们，你们是怎么到这里的？”
[S6] 煤回答说：“我幸运地从火中跳了出来，否则我肯定会死掉。我会被烧成灰烬。”
[S7] 豆子说：“我也完好无损地逃了出来。如果老妇人把我留在锅里，我会像我的同伴们一样被煮成软糊。”
[S8] 稻草说：“我的命运也不会好到哪里去。”
[S9] “老妇人把我的兄弟们变成了火和烟，她一下子夺走了六十个兄弟的生命。非常幸运的是，我设法从她的指缝中溜了出来。”
[S10] 煤说：“我们现在最好做什么？”
[S11] 豆子回答说：“我想，既然我们如此幸运地逃过一劫，我们应该团结在一起。为了避免在这里遭遇更多不幸，我们应该去国外。”
evidence_context_zh: [S4] 很快一块炽热的煤从火中跳出来加入了它们。
